role: A HUD region component that renders the live run-state header and the chronological event feed for the current activity.
capabilities: Display current graph name and live state counts as tone-coded badges
Render the event feed using the shared .ev row vocabulary
Defensively coerce structured or null event summaries into one-line strings
Format event timestamps as relative time via relTime
Key feed rows by event sequence number with index fallback
substance_why: It implements a complete region with live-state header, mapped event list, defensive summary coercion, and shared styling hooks.
interactions_missing: No event row selection, expansion, or detail view
No filtering, searching, or sorting of the event feed
No virtualization, pagination, or truncation for long event lists
No user actions such as clearing, copying, or pinning events
No loading, error, or empty-state interactions beyond a static muted message
performance_risks: Unbounded events.map without virtualization or length cap can degrade with large feeds
No memoization of the event list, summaryText results, or relTime formatting
Fallback keying by array index when e.seq is missing can hurt reconciliation
JSON.stringify of large structured summaries on every render is expensive
reuse_why: The region's responsibilities and defensive summary logic are worth keeping, but its layout and event-row presentation should be rebuilt in the new design language.
decomposition_hints: Split the HEAD live-state summary into its own component (e.g., ActivityHead)
Split the FEED event list and individual event row into separate components
Extract summaryText into a shared event-formatting utility
Move empty-state rendering into a dedicated placeholder component
